text.title: ai-design-assistant-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-design-assistant-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sidebar assistants degrade when the user's working context changes faster than the context window updates. The assistant gives stale suggestions for the previous selection state.

failure-mode.description: Review assistant without a confidence threshold filter.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Review assistants produce false positives at scale. Without a confidence threshold, noise overwhelms useful signal and designers stop trusting the tool.

failure-mode.description: Documentation assistant on files with non-standard layer naming.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Documentation assistants hallucinate component descriptions when design files use non-descriptive layer names (e.g., "Frame 23", "Group 5"). Descriptive naming is a prerequisite for reliable doc generation.

failure-mode.description: Inline assistant that batch-applies node property changes without undo hooks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents that batch-apply Figma changes without going through the undo stack can corrupt files. All AI design actions must be reversible via Figma's undo mechanism.

failure-mode.description: UXP plugin making direct HTTP calls to Claude API.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: UXP plugin sandbox does not allow external HTTP requests by default. Calls to Claude must proxy through a backend service.
